anonymized_passage: before classifying a new content item, before the item's harmfulness and classifier error is known, a moderation policy owner sets a review threshold using privacy-permitted signals using approved signal features, error costs, and reviewer capacity. The stated objective is to maximize a different social-welfare objective that the model does not measure, subject to the condition that privacy-restricted attributes are unavailable unless consent and policy permit their use. The threshold routes uncertain items to review and changes false-positive and false-negative exposure. More review reduces classification harm but consumes scarce reviewer attention. The proposal does not treat revenue as welfare or assume unrestricted access to sensitive data. The study asks how privacy limits alter the value of selective human review.

model_or_study_facts: privacy-restricted attributes are unavailable unless consent and policy permit their use
the item's harmfulness and classifier error
decisions: sets a review threshold using privacy-permitted signals
context.objective: minimize expected moderation harm and review cost
context.timing: before classifying a new content item
decision_time_information: approved signal features, error costs, and reviewer capacity

supplied_evidence: Synthetic illustration; no external empirical evidence supplied.
